931	/// The different kinds of data in the cache are stored in different bucket, which in our case
932	/// are subdirectories of the cache root.
933	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, Eq, PartialEq, Hash)]
934	pub enum CacheBucket {
935	    /// Wheels (excluding built wheels), alongside their metadata and cache policy.
936	    ///
937	    /// There are three kinds from cache entries: Wheel metadata and policy as `MsgPack` files, the
938	    /// wheels themselves, and the unzipped wheel archives. If a wheel file is over an in-memory
939	    /// size threshold, we first download the zip file into the cache, then unzip it into a
940	    /// directory with the same name (exclusive of the `.whl` extension).
941	    ///
942	    /// Cache structure:
943	    ///  * `wheel-metadata-v0/pypi/foo/{foo-1.0.0-py3-none-any.msgpack, foo-1.0.0-py3-none-any.whl}`
944	    ///  * `wheel-metadata-v0/<digest(index-url)>/foo/{foo-1.0.0-py3-none-any.msgpack, foo-1.0.0-py3-none-any.whl}`
945	    ///  * `wheel-metadata-v0/url/<digest(url)>/foo/{foo-1.0.0-py3-none-any.msgpack, foo-1.0.0-py3-none-any.whl}`
946	    ///
947	    /// See `uv_client::RegistryClient::wheel_metadata` for information on how wheel metadata
948	    /// is fetched.
949	    ///
950	    /// # Example
951	    ///
952	    /// Consider the following `requirements.in`:
953	    /// ```text
954	    /// # pypi wheel
955	    /// pandas
956	    /// # url wheel
957	    /// flask @ https://files.pythonhosted.org/packages/36/42/015c23096649b908c809c69388a805a571a3bea44362fe87e33fc3afa01f/flask-3.0.0-py3-none-any.whl
958	    /// ```
959	    ///
960	    /// When we run `pip compile`, it will only fetch and cache the metadata (and cache policy), it
961	    /// doesn't need the actual wheels yet:
962	    /// ```text
963	    /// wheel-v0
964	    /// ├── pypi
965	    /// │   ...
966	    /// │   ├── pandas
967	    /// │   │   └── pandas-2.1.3-cp310-cp310-manylinux_2_17_x86_64.manylinux2014_x86_64.msgpack
968	    /// │   ...
969	    /// └── url
970	    ///     └── 4b8be67c801a7ecb
971	    ///         └── flask
972	    ///             └── flask-3.0.0-py3-none-any.msgpack
973	    /// ```
974	    ///
975	    /// We get the following `requirement.txt` from `pip compile`:
976	    ///
977	    /// ```text
978	    /// [...]
979	    /// flask @ https://files.pythonhosted.org/packages/36/42/015c23096649b908c809c69388a805a571a3bea44362fe87e33fc3afa01f/flask-3.0.0-py3-none-any.whl
980	    /// [...]
981	    /// pandas==2.1.3
982	    /// [...]
983	    /// ```
984	    ///
985	    /// If we run `pip sync` on `requirements.txt` on a different machine, it also fetches the
986	    /// wheels:
987	    ///
988	    /// TODO(konstin): This is still wrong, we need to store the cache policy too!
989	    /// ```text
990	    /// wheel-v0
991	    /// ├── pypi
992	    /// │   ...
993	    /// │   ├── pandas
994	    /// │   │   ├── pandas-2.1.3-cp310-cp310-manylinux_2_17_x86_64.manylinux2014_x86_64.whl
995	    /// │   │   ├── pandas-2.1.3-cp310-cp310-manylinux_2_17_x86_64.manylinux2014_x86_64
996	    /// │   ...
997	    /// └── url
998	    ///     └── 4b8be67c801a7ecb
999	    ///         └── flask
1000	    ///             └── flask-3.0.0-py3-none-any.whl
1001	    ///                 ├── flask
1002	    ///                 │   └── ...
1003	    ///                 └── flask-3.0.0.dist-info
1004	    ///                     └── ...
1005	    /// ```
1006	    ///
1007	    /// If we run first `pip compile` and then `pip sync` on the same machine, we get both:
1008	    ///
1009	    /// ```text
1010	    /// wheels-v0
1011	    /// ├── pypi
1012	    /// │   ├── ...
1013	    /// │   ├── pandas
1014	    /// │   │   ├── pandas-2.1.3-cp312-cp312-manylinux_2_17_x86_64.manylinux2014_x86_64.msgpack
1015	    /// │   │   ├── pandas-2.1.3-cp312-cp312-manylinux_2_17_x86_64.manylinux2014_x86_64.whl
1016	    /// │   │   └── pandas-2.1.3-cp312-cp312-manylinux_2_17_x86_64.manylinux2014_x86_64
1017	    /// │   │       ├── pandas
1018	    /// │   │       │   ├── ...
1019	    /// │   │       ├── pandas-2.1.3.dist-info
1020	    /// │   │       │   ├── ...
1021	    /// │   │       └── pandas.libs
1022	    /// │   ├── ...
1023	    /// └── url
1024	    ///     └── 4b8be67c801a7ecb
1025	    ///         └── flask
1026	    ///             ├── flask-3.0.0-py3-none-any.msgpack
1027	    ///             ├── flask-3.0.0-py3-none-any.msgpack
1028	    ///             └── flask-3.0.0-py3-none-any
1029	    ///                 ├── flask
1030	    ///                 │   └── ...
1031	    ///                 └── flask-3.0.0.dist-info
1032	    ///                     └── ...
1033	    Wheels,
1034	    /// Source distributions, wheels built from source distributions, their extracted metadata, and the
1035	    /// cache policy of the source distribution.
1036	    ///
1037	    /// The structure is similar of that of the `Wheel` bucket, except we have an additional layer
1038	    /// for the source distribution filename and the metadata is at the source distribution-level,
1039	    /// not at the wheel level.
1040	    ///
1041	    /// TODO(konstin): The cache policy should be on the source distribution level, the metadata we
1042	    /// can put next to the wheels as in the `Wheels` bucket.
1043	    ///
1044	    /// The unzipped source distribution is stored in a directory matching the source distribution
1045	    /// archive name.
1046	    ///
1047	    /// Source distributions are built into zipped wheel files (as PEP 517 specifies) and unzipped
1048	    /// lazily before installing. So when resolving, we only build the wheel and store the archive
1049	    /// file in the cache, when installing, we unpack it under the same name (exclusive of the
1050	    /// `.whl` extension). You may find a mix of wheel archive zip files and unzipped wheel
1051	    /// directories in the cache.
1052	    ///
1053	    /// Cache structure:
1054	    ///  * `built-wheels-v0/pypi/foo/34a17436ed1e9669/{manifest.msgpack, metadata.msgpack, foo-1.0.0.zip, foo-1.0.0-py3-none-any.whl, ...other wheels}`
1055	    ///  * `built-wheels-v0/<digest(index-url)>/foo/foo-1.0.0.zip/{manifest.msgpack, metadata.msgpack, foo-1.0.0-py3-none-any.whl, ...other wheels}`
1056	    ///  * `built-wheels-v0/url/<digest(url)>/foo/foo-1.0.0.zip/{manifest.msgpack, metadata.msgpack, foo-1.0.0-py3-none-any.whl, ...other wheels}`
1057	    ///  * `built-wheels-v0/git/<digest(url)>/<git sha>/foo/foo-1.0.0.zip/{metadata.msgpack, foo-1.0.0-py3-none-any.whl, ...other wheels}`
1058	    ///
1059	    /// But the url filename does not need to be a valid source dist filename
1060	    /// (<https://github.com/search?q=path%3A**%2Frequirements.txt+master.zip&type=code>),
1061	    /// so it could also be the following and we have to take any string as filename:
1062	    ///  * `built-wheels-v0/url/<sha256(url)>/master.zip/metadata.msgpack`
1063	    ///
1064	    /// # Example
1065	    ///
1066	    /// The following requirements:
1067	    /// ```text
1068	    /// # git source dist
1069	    /// pydantic-extra-types @ git+https://github.com/pydantic/pydantic-extra-types.git
1070	    /// # pypi source dist
1071	    /// django_allauth==0.51.0
1072	    /// # url source dist
1073	    /// werkzeug @ https://files.pythonhosted.org/packages/0d/cc/ff1904eb5eb4b455e442834dabf9427331ac0fa02853bf83db817a7dd53d/werkzeug-3.0.1.tar.gz
1074	    /// ```
1075	    ///
1076	    /// ...may be cached as:
1077	    /// ```text
1078	    /// built-wheels-v4/
1079	    /// ├── git
1080	    /// │   └── 2122faf3e081fb7a
1081	    /// │       └── 7a2d650a4a7b4d04
1082	    /// │           ├── metadata.msgpack
1083	    /// │           └── pydantic_extra_types-2.9.0-py3-none-any.whl
1084	    /// ├── pypi
1085	    /// │   └── django-allauth
1086	    /// │       └── 0.51.0
1087	    /// │           ├── 0gH-_fwv8tdJ7JwwjJsUc
1088	    /// │           │   ├── django-allauth-0.51.0.tar.gz
1089	    /// │           │   │   └── [UNZIPPED CONTENTS]
1090	    /// │           │   ├── django_allauth-0.51.0-py3-none-any.whl
1091	    /// │           │   └── metadata.msgpack
1092	    /// │           └── revision.http
1093	    /// └── url
1094	    ///     └── 6781bd6440ae72c2
1095	    ///         ├── APYY01rbIfpAo_ij9sCY6
1096	    ///         │   ├── metadata.msgpack
1097	    ///         │   ├── werkzeug-3.0.1-py3-none-any.whl
1098	    ///         │   └── werkzeug-3.0.1.tar.gz
1099	    ///         │       └── [UNZIPPED CONTENTS]
1100	    ///         └── revision.http
1101	    /// ```
1102	    ///
1103	    /// Structurally, the `manifest.msgpack` is empty, and only contains the caching information
1104	    /// needed to invalidate the cache. The `metadata.msgpack` contains the metadata of the source
1105	    /// distribution.
1106	    SourceDistributions,
1107	    /// Flat index responses, a format very similar to the simple metadata API.
1108	    ///
1109	    /// Cache structure:
1110	    ///  * `flat-index-v0/index/<digest(flat_index_url)>.msgpack`
1111	    ///
1112	    /// The response is stored as `Vec<File>`.
1113	    FlatIndex,
1114	    /// Git repositories.
1115	    Git,
1116	    /// Information about an interpreter at a path.
1117	    ///
1118	    /// To avoid caching pyenv shims, bash scripts which may redirect to a new python version
1119	    /// without the shim itself changing, we only cache when the path equals `sys.executable`, i.e.
1120	    /// the path we're running is the python executable itself and not a shim.
1121	    ///
1122	    /// Cache structure: `interpreter-v0/<digest(path)>.msgpack`
1123	    ///
1124	    /// # Example
1125	    ///
1126	    /// The contents of each of the `MsgPack` files has a timestamp field in unix time, the [PEP 508]
1127	    /// markers and some information from the `sys`/`sysconfig` modules.
1128	    ///
1129	    /// ```json
1130	    /// {
1131	    ///   "timestamp": 1698047994491,
1132	    ///   "data": {
1133	    ///     "markers": {
1134	    ///       "implementation_name": "cpython",
1135	    ///       "implementation_version": "3.12.0",
1136	    ///       "os_name": "posix",
1137	    ///       "platform_machine": "x86_64",

1207	    fn remove(self, cache: &Cache, name: &PackageName) -> Result<Removal, io::Error> {
1208	        /// Returns `true` if the [`Path`] represents a built wheel for the given package.
1209	        fn is_match(path: &Path, name: &PackageName) -> bool {
1210	            let Ok(metadata) = fs_err::read(path.join("metadata.msgpack")) else {
1211	                return false;
1212	            };
1213	            let Ok(metadata) = rmp_serde::from_slice::<ResolutionMetadata>(&metadata) else {
1214	                return false;
1215	            };
1216	            metadata.name == *name
1217	        }
1218	
1219	        let mut summary = Removal::default();
1220	        match self {
1221	            Self::Wheels => {
1222	                // For `pypi` wheels, we expect a directory per package (indexed by name).
1223	                let root = cache.bucket(self).join(WheelCacheKind::Pypi);
1224	                summary += rm_rf(root.join(name.to_string()))?;
1225	
1226	                // For alternate indices, we expect a directory for every index (under an `index`
1227	                // subdirectory), followed by a directory per package (indexed by name).
1228	                let root = cache.bucket(self).join(WheelCacheKind::Index);
1229	                for directory in directories(root)? {
1230	                    summary += rm_rf(directory.join(name.to_string()))?;
1231	                }
1232	
1233	                // For direct URLs, we expect a directory for every URL, followed by a
1234	                // directory per package (indexed by name).
1235	                let root = cache.bucket(self).join(WheelCacheKind::Url);
1236	                for directory in directories(root)? {
1237	                    summary += rm_rf(directory.join(name.to_string()))?;
1238	                }
1239	            }
1240	            Self::SourceDistributions => {
1241	                // For `pypi` wheels, we expect a directory per package (indexed by name).
1242	                let root = cache.bucket(self).join(WheelCacheKind::Pypi);
1243	                summary += rm_rf(root.join(name.to_string()))?;
1244	
1245	                // For alternate indices, we expect a directory for every index (under an `index`
1246	                // subdirectory), followed by a directory per package (indexed by name).
1247	                let root = cache.bucket(self).join(WheelCacheKind::Index);
1248	                for directory in directories(root)? {
1249	                    summary += rm_rf(directory.join(name.to_string()))?;
1250	                }
1251	
1252	                // For direct URLs, we expect a directory for every URL, followed by a
1253	                // directory per version. To determine whether the URL is relevant, we need to
1254	                // search for a wheel matching the package name.
1255	                let root = cache.bucket(self).join(WheelCacheKind::Url);
1256	                for url in directories(root)? {
1257	                    if directories(&url)?.any(|version| is_match(&version, name)) {
1258	                        summary += rm_rf(url)?;
1259	                    }
1260	                }
1261	
1262	                // For local dependencies, we expect a directory for every path, followed by a
1263	                // directory per version. To determine whether the path is relevant, we need to
1264	                // search for a wheel matching the package name.
1265	                let root = cache.bucket(self).join(WheelCacheKind::Path);
1266	                for path in directories(root)? {
1267	                    if directories(&path)?.any(|version| is_match(&version, name)) {
1268	                        summary += rm_rf(path)?;
1269	                    }
1270	                }
1271	
1272	                // For Git dependencies, we expect a directory for every repository, followed by a
1273	                // directory for every SHA. To determine whether the SHA is relevant, we need to
1274	                // search for a wheel matching the package name.
1275	                let root = cache.bucket(self).join(WheelCacheKind::Git);
1276	                for repository in directories(root)? {
1277	                    for sha in directories(repository)? {
1278	                        if is_match(&sha, name) {
1279	                            summary += rm_rf(sha)?;
1280	                        }
1281	                    }
1282	                }
1283	            }
1284	            Self::Simple => {
1285	                // For `pypi` wheels, we expect a rkyv file per package, indexed by name.
1286	                let root = cache.bucket(self).join(WheelCacheKind::Pypi);
1287	                summary += rm_rf(root.join(format!("{name}.rkyv")))?;
1288	
1289	                // For alternate indices, we expect a directory for every index (under an `index`
1290	                // subdirectory), followed by a directory per package (indexed by name).
1291	                let root = cache.bucket(self).join(WheelCacheKind::Index);
1292	                for directory in directories(root)? {
1293	                    summary += rm_rf(directory.join(format!("{name}.rkyv")))?;
1294	                }
1295	            }
1296	            Self::FlatIndex => {
1297	                // We can't know if the flat index includes a package, so we just remove the entire
1298	                // cache entry.
1299	                let root = cache.bucket(self);
1300	                summary += rm_rf(root)?;
1301	            }
1302	            Self::Git
1303	            | Self::Interpreter
1304	            | Self::Archive
1305	            | Self::Builds
1306	            | Self::Environments
1307	            | Self::Python
1308	            | Self::Binaries => {

1310	            }
1311	        }

888	    /// `%LOCALAPPDATA%\uv\cache` on Windows.
889	    ///

893	    /// An alternative cache directory may be specified via the `cache-dir` setting, the
894	    /// `--cache-dir` option, or the `$UV_CACHE_DIR` environment variable.
895	    ///
896	    /// Note that it is important for performance for the cache directory to be located on the same
897	    /// file system as the Python environment uv is operating on.

901	    /// Displays the total size of the cache directory. This includes all downloaded and built

904	    Size(SizeArgs),
905	}

922	pub struct PruneArgs {
923	    /// Optimize the cache for persistence in a continuous integration environment, like GitHub
924	    /// Actions.
925	    ///
926	    /// By default, uv caches both the wheels that it builds from source and the pre-built wheels
927	    /// that it downloads directly, to enable high-performance package installation. In some
928	    /// scenarios, though, persisting pre-built wheels may be undesirable. For example, in GitHub
929	    /// Actions, it's faster to omit pre-built wheels from the cache and instead have re-download
930	    /// them on each run. However, it typically _is_ faster to cache wheels that are built from
931	    /// source, since the wheel building process can be expensive, especially for extension
932	    /// modules.
933	    ///
934	    /// In `--ci` mode, uv will prune any pre-built wheels from the cache, but retain any wheels
935	    /// that were built from source.
936	    #[arg(long)]
937	    pub ci: bool,
938	
939	    /// Force removal of the cache, ignoring in-use checks.
940	    ///
941	    /// By default, `uv cache prune` will block until no process is reading the cache. When
942	    /// `--force` is used, `uv cache prune` will proceed without taking a lock.
943	    #[arg(long)]
944	    pub force: bool,
945	}

11	/// When the cache directory cannot be created (e.g., due to permissions), we should show a
12	/// chained error message that indicates we failed to initialize the cache.
13	#[test]
14	#[cfg(unix)]
15	fn cache_init_failure() -> Result<()> {
16	    use uv_test::ReadOnlyDirectoryGuard;
17	
18	    let context = uv_test::test_context!("3.12");
19	
20	    let pyproject_toml = context.temp_dir.child("pyproject.toml");
21	    pyproject_toml.write_str(
22	        r#"
23	        [project]
24	        name = "project"
25	        version = "0.1.0"
26	        requires-python = ">=3.12"
27	        dependencies = ["iniconfig"]
28	        "#,
29	    )?;
30	
31	    // Create a read-only directory that will serve as the parent of the cache.
32	    // The guard sets it to read-only and restores original permissions on drop (including panic).
33	    let cache_parent = context.temp_dir.child("cache_parent");
34	    fs_err::create_dir(&cache_parent)?;
35	    let _guard = ReadOnlyDirectoryGuard::new(cache_parent.path())?;
36	
37	    // Point the cache to a subdirectory within the read-only parent
38	    let cache_dir = cache_parent.child("cache");
39	
40	    // Filter both the relative path (in the first line) and absolute path (in the cause)
41	    let context = context
42	        .with_filter((r"cache_parent/cache", "[CACHE_DIR]"))
43	        .with_filter((
44	            r"failed to create directory `.*`",
45	            "failed to create directory `[CACHE_DIR]`",
46	        ));
47	
48	    // Build the sync command manually to use our custom cache directory.
49	    // We can't use context.sync() because it adds --cache-dir with the default cache.
50	    let mut command = Command::new(get_bin!());
51	    command
52	        .arg("sync")
53	        .arg("--cache-dir")
54	        .arg(cache_dir.path())
55	        .current_dir(context.temp_dir.path());
56	    context.add_shared_env(&mut command, false);
57	
58	    // Running a command should fail with a chained error about cache initialization
59	    uv_snapshot!(context.filters(), command, @"
60	    success: false
61	    exit_code: 2
62	    ----- stdout -----
63	
64	    ----- stderr -----
65	    error: Failed to initialize cache at `[CACHE_DIR]`
66	      Caused by: failed to create directory `[CACHE_DIR]`: Permission denied (os error 13)
67	    ");
68	
69	    Ok(())
70	}

Perfect! Now I have all the information I need. Let me create a comprehensive summary for the user.

## Comprehensive Cache Bucket Structure Analysis

I've completed a thorough analysis of the cache bucket structure in the uv Rust codebase. Here's what I found:

---

### 1. **CacheBucket Enum Definition**

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775506119648-jjxuk0/crates/uv-cache/src/lib.rs`

**Lines:** 931-1177

The `CacheBucket` enum defines 11 distinct cache bucket types:

```rust
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, Eq, PartialEq, Hash)]
pub enum CacheBucket {
    /// Wheels (excluding built wheels), metadata, cache policy
    Wheels,
    
    /// Source distributions, built wheels from source, metadata, cache policy
    SourceDistributions,
    
    /// Flat index responses (simple metadata API format)
    FlatIndex,
    
    /// Git repositories
    Git,
    
    /// Interpreter information at a path
    Interpreter,
    
    /// Index responses through simple metadata API
    Simple,
    
    /// Unzipped wheels stored as directories (internal use)
    Archive,
    
    /// Ephemeral virtual environments for PEP 517 builds
    Builds,
    
    /// Reusable virtual environments for invoking Python tools
    Environments,
    
    /// Cached Python downloads
    Python,
    
    /// Downloaded tool binaries (e.g., Ruff)
    Binaries,
}
```

---

### 2. **Cache Directory Structure**

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775506119648-jjxuk0/crates/uv-cache/src/lib.rs`

**Lines:** 1179-1202 (the `to_str()` implementation)

Each bucket is mapped to a versioned directory name:

| Bucket | Directory Name |
|--------|----------------|
| `Wheels` | `wheels-v6` |
| `SourceDistributions` | `sdists-v9` |
| `FlatIndex` | `flat-index-v2` |
| `Git` | `git-v0` |
| `Interpreter` | `interpreter-v4` |
| `Simple` | `simple-v21` |
| `Archive` | `archive-v0` |
| `Builds` | `builds-v0` |
| `Environments` | `environments-v2` |
| `Python` | `python-v0` |
| `Binaries` | `binaries-v0` |

**Directory Layout Example:**
```
~/.cache/uv/
├── CACHEDIR.TAG
├── .gitignore
├── .git (phony)
├── .lock
├── wheels-v6/
│   ├── pypi/
│   ├── index/
│   ├── url/
│   ├── path/
│   ├── editable/
│   └── git/
├── sdists-v9/
│   ├── pypi/
│   ├── index/
│   ├── url/
│   ├── path/
│   └── git/
├── flat-index-v2/
├── git-v0/
├── interpreter-v4/
├── simple-v21/
├── archive-v0/
├── builds-v0/
├── environments-v2/
├── python-v0/
└── binaries-v0/
```

---

### 3. **Cache Struct Implementation**

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775506119648-jjxuk0/crates/uv-cache/src/lib.rs`

**Lines:** 160-503 (Cache struct and initialization)

```rust
pub struct Cache {
    root: PathBuf,
    refresh: Refresh,
    temp_dir: Option<Arc<tempfile::TempDir>>,
    lock_file: Option<Arc<LockedFile>>,
}
```

**Key Methods:**

1. **`bucket(cache_bucket: CacheBucket) -> PathBuf`** (Line 281)
   - Returns the full path to a specific cache bucket directory
   
2. **`shard(cache_bucket: CacheBucket, dir: impl AsRef<Path>) -> CacheShard`** (Line 286)
   - Computes a subdirectory within a cache bucket

3. **`entry(cache_bucket: CacheBucket, dir, file) -> CacheEntry`** (Line 291)
   - Computes an entry path within a cache bucket

4. **`init(self) -> Result<Self, Error>`** (Line 470)
   - Initializes cache with base files (.gitignore, CACHEDIR.TAG, .git, .lock)

5. **`clear(self, reporter) -> Result<Removal, io::Error>`** (Line 527)
   - Clears all cache entries except the lock file

6. **`remove(name: &PackageName) -> Result<Removal, io::Error>`** (Line 560)
   - Removes all entries for a specific package from all buckets

7. **`prune(ci: bool) -> Result<Removal, io::Error>`** (Line 582)
   - Garbage collects the cache, removing dangling entries

---

### 4. **WheelCacheKind Sub-Structure**

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775506119648-jjxuk0/crates/uv-cache/src/wheel.rs`

**Lines:** 56-86

The `Wheels` and `SourceDistributions` buckets further subdivide into source kinds:

```rust
pub(crate) enum WheelCacheKind {
    Pypi,       // "pypi"
    Index,      // "index"
    Url,        // "url"
    Path,       // "path"
    Editable,   // "editable"
    Git,        // "git"
}
```

This creates a hierarchical structure within wheels/sdists buckets, allowing organization by source type.

---

### 5. **CacheBucket Iterator**

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775506119648-jjxuk0/crates/uv-cache/src/lib.rs`

**Lines:** 1316-1331

```rust
pub fn iter() -> impl Iterator<Item = Self> {
    [
        Self::Wheels,
        Self::SourceDistributions,
        Self::FlatIndex,
        Self::Git,
        Self::Interpreter,
        Self::Simple,
        Self::Archive,
        Self::Builds,
        Self::Environments,
        Self::Binaries,
    ]
    .iter()
    .copied()
}
```

Note: `Python` is intentionally excluded from the iterator (but defined in the enum). This is likely because Python downloads have different handling logic.

---

### 6. **Cache Removal Logic**

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775506119648-jjxuk0/crates/uv-cache/src/lib.rs`

**Lines:** 1204-1313

The `remove()` method (lines 1207-1313) iterates through buckets differently based on structure:

- **Wheels & SourceDistributions:** Search across pypi/, index/{digest}/, url/{digest}/, path/{digest}/, git/{digest}/{sha}/
- **Simple:** Search pypi/ and index/{digest}/
- **FlatIndex:** Removes entire bucket (can't know if it includes a package)
- **Git, Interpreter, Archive, Builds, Environments, Python, Binaries:** Do nothing (no package-specific removal)

---

### 7. **Removal Statistics Struct**

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775506119648-jjxuk0/crates/uv-cache/src/removal.rs**

**Lines:** 43-55

```rust
pub struct Removal {
    pub num_files: u64,
    pub num_dirs: u64,
    pub total_bytes: u64,
}
```

Tracks statistics when cache entries are removed.

---

### 8. **Cache Size Computation**

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775506119648-jjxuk0/crates/uv/src/commands/cache_size.rs`

**Lines:** 13-47

Uses the `diskus` crate to recursively calculate disk usage of the entire cache root:

```rust
let disk_usage = DiskUsage::new(vec![cache.root().to_path_buf()]);
let total_bytes = disk_usage.count_ignoring_errors();
```

---

### 9. **Archive Bucket (Special Purpose)**

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775506119648-jjxuk0/crates/uv-cache/src/archive.rs`

**Lines:** 4-39

The Archive bucket stores unzipped wheels as directories with unique IDs (nanoid-based):

```rust
pub struct ArchiveId(String);

impl ArchiveId {
    pub fn new() -> Self {
        Self(nanoid::nanoid!())
    }
}
```

Other buckets symlink to archive entries for atomic operations.

---

### 10. **Initialization & Base Files**

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775506119648-jjxuk0/crates/uv-cache/src/lib.rs`

**Lines:** 407-467

When initializing, the cache creates:
- `CACHEDIR.TAG` - Standard cache directory marker
- `.gitignore` - Contains `*` to ignore all cache contents
- `.git` - Phony file to prevent builds from treating cache as git repo
- `.lock` - File locking mechanism for concurrent access control
- `sdists-v9/.gitignore` - Empty to prevent parent .gitignore interference

---

## Summary

The cache is organized as a two-level hierarchy:

1. **Bucket Level**: 11 distinct bucket types, each versioned (e.g., `wheels-v6`, `sdists-v9`)
2. **Source Type Level** (within Wheels/SourceDistributions): Further divided by source (PyPI, custom index, URL, path, git)

This design allows:
- Isolated cache management per data type
- Version-safe upgrades (old versions kept, new versions created)
- Atomic operations via archive bucket symlinks
- Concurrent access via lock file mechanism
- Efficient package-specific removal operations

## Summary

The cache is organized as a two-level hierarchy:

1. **Bucket Level**: 11 distinct bucket types, each versioned (e.g., `wheels-v6`, `sdists-v9`)
2. **Source Type Level** (within Wheels/SourceDistributions): Further divided by source (PyPI, custom index, URL, path, git)

This design allows:
- Isolated cache management per data type
- Version-safe upgrades (old versions kept, new versions created)
- Atomic operations via archive bucket symlinks
- Concurrent access via lock file mechanism
- Efficient package-specific removal operations

## Summary: Output Formatting Patterns in the UV Rust Codebase

Based on my thorough analysis of the codebase, here are the key findings about output formatting patterns:

### 1. **`uv cache size` Output Formatting**

The `cache_size` command has two output modes controlled by the `--human` / `-H` / `--human-readable` flag:

**Files involved:**
- `/crates/uv/src/commands/cache_size.rs` - Main implementation
- `/crates/uv-cli/src/lib.rs` - CLI argument definitions (SizeArgs struct)

**Output patterns:**
- **Raw bytes (default):** Just the number `0` or `12345678`
- **Human-readable (with --human flag):** Formatted as `1.2 GiB`, `384.2 KiB`, etc.

Code example from cache_size.rs (lines 27-44):
```rust
if human_readable {
    let (bytes, unit) = human_readable_bytes(total_bytes);
    writeln!(printer.stdout_important(), "{bytes:.1}{unit}")?;
} else {
    writeln!(printer.stdout_important(), "{total_bytes}")?;
}
```

### 2. **Human-Readable Size Formatting Utility**

**Location:** `/crates/uv-console/src/lib.rs` (lines 300-314)

The core formatting function:
```rust
pub fn human_readable_bytes(bytes: u64) -> (f32, &'static str) {
    const UNITS: [&str; 7] = ["B", "KiB", "MiB", "GiB", "TiB", "PiB", "EiB"];
    let bytes_f32 = bytes as f32;
    let i = ((bytes_f32.log2() / 10.0) as usize).min(UNITS.len() - 1);
    (bytes_f32 / 1024_f32.powi(i as i32), UNITS[i])
}
```

**Key characteristics:**
- Uses binary units (1024-based, not 1000-based SI units)
- Returns tuple of `(f32, unit_string)`
- Formatted with `.1` precision (one decimal place): `{bytes:.1}{unit}`
- Units: B, KiB, MiB, GiB, TiB, PiB, EiB

**Usage across commands:**
- `/crates/uv/src/commands/cache_size.rs`
- `/crates/uv/src/commands/cache_clean.rs`
- `/crates/uv/src/commands/cache_prune.rs`
- `/crates/uv/src/commands/publish.rs`
- `/crates/uv/src/commands/reporters.rs`

### 3. **Other Commands with --human Flags**

**Commands with similar patterns:**
- `cache clean` - Also uses `human_readable_bytes()` for summary output
- `cache prune` - Also uses `human_readable_bytes()` for summary output
- Both format total bytes with conditional logic (lines 95-100 in both files)

Special handling for small files (< 1024 bytes):
```rust
let bytes = if summary.total_bytes < 1024 {
    format!("{}B", summary.total_bytes)
} else {
    let (bytes, unit) = human_readable_bytes(summary.total_bytes);
    format!("{bytes:.1}{unit}")
};
```

### 4. **Output/Printing Module Architecture**

**Location:** `/crates/uv/src/printer.rs`

The `Printer` enum controls output:
```rust
pub(crate) enum Printer {
    Silent,      // Suppresses all output
    Quiet,       // Preserves "important" stdout only
    Default,     // Standard output to stdout/stderr
    Verbose,     // All output including debug messages
    NoProgress,  // Standard output, no progress bars
}
```

**Output methods:**
- `printer.stdout()` - For standard output
- `printer.stdout_important()` - For output that shows even in quiet mode
- `printer.stderr()` - For standard error
- Both implement `std::fmt::Write` trait

### 5. **Structured Output Patterns (JSON, etc.)**

**Format enums in CLI** (`/crates/uv-cli/src/lib.rs`):
- `VersionFormat` - Text or Json
- `PythonListFormat` - Text or Json
- `SyncFormat` - Text or Json
- `ListFormat` - Columns, Freeze, or Json

**Examples:**
- `/crates/uv/src/commands/project/version.rs` - Uses `serde_json::to_string_pretty()`
- `/crates/uv/src/commands/python/list.rs` - Uses `serde_json::to_string()`
- `/crates/uv/src/commands/project/sync.rs` - JSON formatting for structured output

Note: `cache size` command does NOT currently have JSON output format support (only raw bytes or human-readable)

### 6. **Cache Command Serialization Patterns**

**Location:** `/crates/uv-cache/src/lib.rs`

Cache-related structs typically use:
```rust
#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub struct Cache { ... }

#[derive(Debug)]
pub struct Removal {
    pub num_files: u64,
    pub num_dirs: u64,
    pub total_bytes: u64,
}
```

These are NOT serialized to JSON by default - they're used internally and output as formatted text.

### 7. **Test Patterns**

**Location:** `/crates/uv/tests/it/cache_size.rs`

Test examples show:
- Empty cache: outputs `0` (raw) or `0B` (human-readable)
- Populated cache: outputs raw byte count or human-readable size
- Tests use snapshot matching with `[SIZE]` placeholder for filtering actual byte counts

### 8. **Display/Color Patterns**

Commands use the `owo_colors` crate for styled output:
```rust
use owo_colors::OwoColorize;

writeln!(printer.stderr(), "Cleared cache at: {}", 
    cache.root().user_display().cyan())?;
```

**Summary of patterns:**
- Cyan for paths
- Green for success messages
- Dimmed for supplementary info

### Key Files for Reference:
1. `/crates/uv/src/commands/cache_size.rs` - Main cache_size implementation
2. `/crates/uv-cli/src/lib.rs` - SizeArgs CLI definitions
3. `/crates/uv-console/src/lib.rs` - human_readable_bytes utility
4. `/crates/uv/src/printer.rs` - Output/printing infrastructure
5. `/crates/uv/tests/it/cache_size.rs` - Test examples

- `/crates/uv/src/commands/cache_size.rs` - Main implementation
- `/crates/uv-cli/src/lib.rs` - CLI argument definitions (SizeArgs struct)

**Output patterns:**
- **Raw bytes (default):** Just the number `0` or `12345678`
- **Human-readable (with --human flag):** Formatted as `1.2 GiB`, `384.2 KiB`, etc.

Code example from cache_size.rs (lines 27-44):
```rust
if human_readable {
    let (bytes, unit) = human_readable_bytes(total_bytes);
    writeln!(printer.stdout_important(), "{bytes:.1}{unit}")?;
} else {
    writeln!(printer.stdout_important(), "{total_bytes}")?;
}
```

### 2. **Human-Readable Size Formatting Utility**

**Location:** `/crates/uv-console/src/lib.rs` (lines 300-314)

The core formatting function:
```rust
pub fn human_readable_bytes(bytes: u64) -> (f32, &'static str) {
    const UNITS: [&str; 7] = ["B", "KiB", "MiB", "GiB", "TiB", "PiB", "EiB"];
    let bytes_f32 = bytes as f32;
    let i = ((bytes_f32.log2() / 10.0) as usize).min(UNITS.len() - 1);
    (bytes_f32 / 1024_f32.powi(i as i32), UNITS[i])
}
```

**Key characteristics:**
- Uses binary units (1024-based, not 1000-based SI units)
- Returns tuple of `(f32, unit_string)`
- Formatted with `.1` precision (one decimal place): `{bytes:.1}{unit}`
- Units: B, KiB, MiB, GiB, TiB, PiB, EiB

**Usage across commands:**
- `/crates/uv/src/commands/cache_size.rs`
- `/crates/uv/src/commands/cache_clean.rs`
- `/crates/uv/src/commands/cache_prune.rs`
- `/crates/uv/src/commands/publish.rs`
- `/crates/uv/src/commands/reporters.rs`

### 3. **Other Commands with --human Flags**

**Commands with similar patterns:**
- `cache clean` - Also uses `human_readable_bytes()` for summary output
- `cache prune` - Also uses `human_readable_bytes()` for summary output
- Both format total bytes with conditional logic (lines 95-100 in both files)

Special handling for small files (< 1024 bytes):
```rust
let bytes = if summary.total_bytes < 1024 {
    format!("{}B", summary.total_bytes)
} else {
    let (bytes, unit) = human_readable_bytes(summary.total_bytes);
    format!("{bytes:.1}{unit}")
};
```

### 4. **Output/Printing Module Architecture**

**Location:** `/crates/uv/src/printer.rs`

The `Printer` enum controls output:
```rust
pub(crate) enum Printer {
    Silent,      // Suppresses all output
    Quiet,       // Preserves "important" stdout only
    Default,     // Standard output to stdout/stderr
    Verbose,     // All output including debug messages
    NoProgress,  // Standard output, no progress bars
}
```

**Output methods:**
- `printer.stdout()` - For standard output
- `printer.stdout_important()` - For output that shows even in quiet mode
- `printer.stderr()` - For standard error
- Both implement `std::fmt::Write` trait

### 5. **Structured Output Patterns (JSON, etc.)**

**Format enums in CLI** (`/crates/uv-cli/src/lib.rs`):
- `VersionFormat` - Text or Json
- `PythonListFormat` - Text or Json
- `SyncFormat` - Text or Json
- `ListFormat` - Columns, Freeze, or Json

**Examples:**
- `/crates/uv/src/commands/project/version.rs` - Uses `serde_json::to_string_pretty()`
- `/crates/uv/src/commands/python/list.rs` - Uses `serde_json::to_string()`
- `/crates/uv/src/commands/project/sync.rs` - JSON formatting for structured output

Note: `cache size` command does NOT currently have JSON output format support (only raw bytes or human-readable)

### 6. **Cache Command Serialization Patterns**

**Location:** `/crates/uv-cache/src/lib.rs`

Cache-related structs typically use:
```rust
#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub struct Cache { ... }

#[derive(Debug)]
pub struct Removal {
    pub num_files: u64,
    pub num_dirs: u64,
    pub total_bytes: u64,
}
```

These are NOT serialized to JSON by default - they're used internally and output as formatted text.

### 7. **Test Patterns**

**Location:** `/crates/uv/tests/it/cache_size.rs`

Test examples show:
- Empty cache: outputs `0` (raw) or `0B` (human-readable)
- Populated cache: outputs raw byte count or human-readable size
- Tests use snapshot matching with `[SIZE]` placeholder for filtering actual byte counts

### 8. **Display/Color Patterns**

Commands use the `owo_colors` crate for styled output:
```rust
use owo_colors::OwoColorize;

writeln!(printer.stderr(), "Cleared cache at: {}", 
    cache.root().user_display().cyan())?;
```

**Summary of patterns:**
- Cyan for paths
- Green for success messages
- Dimmed for supplementary info

### Key Files for Reference:
1. `/crates/uv/src/commands/cache_size.rs` - Main cache_size implementation
2. `/crates/uv-cli/src/lib.rs` - SizeArgs CLI definitions
3. `/crates/uv-console/src/lib.rs` - human_readable_bytes utility
4. `/crates/uv/src/printer.rs` - Output/printing infrastructure
5. `/crates/uv/tests/it/cache_size.rs` - Test examples

1194	            // Note that when bumping this, you'll also need to bump
1195	            // `ARCHIVE_VERSION` in `crates/uv-cache/src/lib.rs`.
1196	            Self::Archive => "archive-v0",
1197	            Self::Builds => "builds-v0",
1198	            Self::Environments => "environments-v2",
1199	            Self::Python => "python-v0",
1200	            Self::Binaries => "binaries-v0",
1201	        }
1202	    }
1203	
1204	    /// Remove a package from the cache bucket.
1205	    ///
1206	    /// Returns the number of entries removed from the cache.
1207	    fn remove(self, cache: &Cache, name: &PackageName) -> Result<Removal, io::Error> {
1208	        /// Returns `true` if the [`Path`] represents a built wheel for the given package.
1209	        fn is_match(path: &Path, name: &PackageName) -> bool {
1210	            let Ok(metadata) = fs_err::read(path.join("metadata.msgpack")) else {
1211	                return false;
1212	            };
1213	            let Ok(metadata) = rmp_serde::from_slice::<ResolutionMetadata>(&metadata) else {
1214	                return false;
1215	            };
1216	            metadata.name == *name
1217	        }
1218	
1219	        let mut summary = Removal::default();
1220	        match self {
1221	            Self::Wheels => {
1222	                // For `pypi` wheels, we expect a directory per package (indexed by name).
1223	                let root = cache.bucket(self).join(WheelCacheKind::Pypi);
1224	                summary += rm_rf(root.join(name.to_string()))?;
1225	
1226	                // For alternate indices, we expect a directory for every index (under an `index`
1227	                // subdirectory), followed by a directory per package (indexed by name).
1228	                let root = cache.bucket(self).join(WheelCacheKind::Index);
1229	                for directory in directories(root)? {
1230	                    summary += rm_rf(directory.join(name.to_string()))?;
1231	                }
1232	
1233	                // For direct URLs, we expect a directory for every URL, followed by a
1234	                // directory per package (indexed by name).
1235	                let root = cache.bucket(self).join(WheelCacheKind::Url);
1236	                for directory in directories(root)? {
1237	                    summary += rm_rf(directory.join(name.to_string()))?;
1238	                }
1239	            }
1240	            Self::SourceDistributions => {
1241	                // For `pypi` wheels, we expect a directory per package (indexed by name).
1242	                let root = cache.bucket(self).join(WheelCacheKind::Pypi);
1243	                summary += rm_rf(root.join(name.to_string()))?;
1244	
1245	                // For alternate indices, we expect a directory for every index (under an `index`
1246	                // subdirectory), followed by a directory per package (indexed by name).
1247	                let root = cache.bucket(self).join(WheelCacheKind::Index);
1248	                for directory in directories(root)? {
1249	                    summary += rm_rf(directory.join(name.to_string()))?;
1250	                }
1251	
1252	                // For direct URLs, we expect a directory for every URL, followed by a
1253	                // directory per version. To determine whether the URL is relevant, we need to
1254	                // search for a wheel matching the package name.
1255	                let root = cache.bucket(self).join(WheelCacheKind::Url);
1256	                for url in directories(root)? {
1257	                    if directories(&url)?.any(|version| is_match(&version, name)) {
1258	                        summary += rm_rf(url)?;
1259	                    }
1260	                }
1261	
1262	                // For local dependencies, we expect a directory for every path, followed by a
1263	                // directory per version. To determine whether the path is relevant, we need to
1264	                // search for a wheel matching the package name.
1265	                let root = cache.bucket(self).join(WheelCacheKind::Path);
1266	                for path in directories(root)? {
1267	                    if directories(&path)?.any(|version| is_match(&version, name)) {
1268	                        summary += rm_rf(path)?;
1269	                    }
1270	                }
1271	
1272	                // For Git dependencies, we expect a directory for every repository, followed by a
1273	                // directory for every SHA. To determine whether the SHA is relevant, we need to
1274	                // search for a wheel matching the package name.
1275	                let root = cache.bucket(self).join(WheelCacheKind::Git);
1276	                for repository in directories(root)? {
1277	                    for sha in directories(repository)? {
1278	                        if is_match(&sha, name) {
1279	                            summary += rm_rf(sha)?;
1280	                        }
1281	                    }
1282	                }
1283	            }
1284	            Self::Simple => {
1285	                // For `pypi` wheels, we expect a rkyv file per package, indexed by name.
1286	                let root = cache.bucket(self).join(WheelCacheKind::Pypi);
1287	                summary += rm_rf(root.join(format!("{name}.rkyv")))?;
1288	
1289	                // For alternate indices, we expect a directory for every index (under an `index`
1290	                // subdirectory), followed by a directory per package (indexed by name).
1291	                let root = cache.bucket(self).join(WheelCacheKind::Index);
1292	                for directory in directories(root)? {
1293	                    summary += rm_rf(directory.join(format!("{name}.rkyv")))?;
1294	                }
1295	            }
1296	            Self::FlatIndex => {
1297	                // We can't know if the flat index includes a package, so we just remove the entire
1298	                // cache entry.
1299	                let root = cache.bucket(self);
1300	                summary += rm_rf(root)?;
1301	            }
1302	            Self::Git
1303	            | Self::Interpreter
1304	            | Self::Archive
1305	            | Self::Builds
1306	            | Self::Environments
1307	            | Self::Python
1308	            | Self::Binaries => {
1309	                // Nothing to do.
1310	            }
1311	        }
1312	        Ok(summary)
1313	    }
1314	
1315	    /// Return an iterator over all cache buckets.
1316	    pub fn iter() -> impl Iterator<Item = Self> {
1317	        [
1318	            Self::Wheels,
1319	            Self::SourceDistributions,
1320	            Self::FlatIndex,
1321	            Self::Git,
1322	            Self::Interpreter,
1323	            Self::Simple,
1324	            Self::Archive,
1325	            Self::Builds,
1326	            Self::Environments,
1327	            Self::Binaries,
1328	        ]
1329	        .iter()
1330	        .copied()
1331	    }
1332	}

1336	        f.write_str(self.to_str())

Design an implementation plan for adding a `uv cache stats` subcommand to the uv Rust project. The command should show per-bucket breakdown of cache usage with a total at the end, supporting a `--human` flag for human-readable sizes.

## Existing Architecture

### CLI Layer (`crates/uv-cli/src/lib.rs`)
- `CacheCommand` enum (line 878) has variants: `Clean`, `Prune`, `Dir`, `Size`
- `SizeArgs` struct (line 947) has a `human: bool` field with `--human`/`-H`/`--human-readable`
- Need to add a new `Stats(StatsArgs)` variant to `CacheCommand`

### Command Dispatch (`crates/uv/src/lib.rs`)
- Line 1179-1181: `CacheCommand::Size(args)` dispatches to `commands::cache_size(&cache, args.human, printer, globals.preview)`
- Need similar dispatch for the new `Stats` variant

### Command Implementation (`crates/uv/src/commands/cache_size.rs`)
- Uses `diskus::DiskUsage` to compute total size of `cache.root()`
- Uses `human_readable_bytes` from `uv_console` for formatting
- Gated behind `PreviewFeature::CacheSize` preview flag
- Outputs to `printer.stdout_important()`

### Cache Buckets (`crates/uv-cache/src/lib.rs`)
- `CacheBucket` enum (line 934) has 11 variants: Wheels, SourceDistributions, FlatIndex, Git, Interpreter, Simple, Archive, Builds, Environments, Python, Binaries
- `CacheBucket::iter()` (line 1316) returns all buckets EXCEPT Python
- `to_str()` maps to directory names: `wheels-v6`, `sdists-v9`, `flat-index-v2`, `git-v0`, `interpreter-v4`, `simple-v21`, `archive-v0`, `builds-v0`, `environments-v2`, `python-v0`, `binaries-v0`
- `Display` impl (line 1334) uses `to_str()` - so displays the versioned directory name
- `cache.bucket(bucket)` returns the PathBuf for a bucket's directory
- `cache.root()` returns the root cache path

### Preview Feature (`crates/uv-preview/src/lib.rs`)
- `PreviewFeature::CacheSize = 1 << 11` - existing preview feature for cache size
- We should reuse this for cache stats OR add a new one

### Commands module (`crates/uv/src/commands/mod.rs`)
- Line 21: `pub(crate) use cache_size::cache_size;`
- Line 84: `mod cache_size;`
- Need to add similar entries for `cache_stats`

### Test Infrastructure
- Tests at `crates/uv/tests/it/cache_size.rs`
- Test module registered in `crates/uv/tests/it/main.rs` line 27
- `TestContext` has `cache_size()` helper at `crates/uv-test/src/lib.rs:1644`
- `with_filtered_cache_size()` at line 241 adds filters for size values in snapshots

### Formatting
- `human_readable_bytes(bytes: u64) -> (f32, &'static str)` from `uv_console` - uses binary units (KiB, MiB, GiB)
- Format: `"{bytes:.1}{unit}"` (e.g., `384.2KiB`)

## Requirements
1. New `uv cache stats` subcommand
2. Per-bucket breakdown showing each bucket name and its size
3. Total at the end
4. `--human` flag for human-readable sizes (same as cache size)
5. Wire through all layers: CLI args → dispatch → implementation → output
6. Reuse `CacheBucket::iter()` and `cache.bucket()` for bucket paths
7. Use `diskus::DiskUsage` for per-bucket disk usage calculation
8. Add integration tests following the pattern of cache_size tests
9. Behind same preview feature as cache size (or add new one)

## Design Considerations
- Should we use `DiskUsage::new()` per bucket (11 separate calls) or compute once and break down?
  - Per-bucket approach is simpler and follows existing pattern
  - Could also compute all bucket paths in one `DiskUsage::new(vec![...all bucket paths...])` call but that gives a single total, not per-bucket
  - Best approach: iterate over buckets, compute each separately with `DiskUsage`, then sum for total
- Output format: consider table-like alignment for readability
- The `CacheBucket::Display` shows versioned dir names like `wheels-v6` - for user-facing output, consider human-friendly names
- `CacheBucket::iter()` excludes Python - should we include it? Probably yes for stats to be accurate

Please design a detailed step-by-step implementation plan with exact files to modify and what changes to make in each.

924	    /// Actions.

926	    /// By default, uv caches both the wheels that it builds from source and the pre-built wheels

928	    /// scenarios, though, persisting pre-built wheels may be undesirable. For example, in GitHub
929	    /// Actions, it's faster to omit pre-built wheels from the cache and instead have re-download
930	    /// them on each run. However, it typically _is_ faster to cache wheels that are built from
931	    /// source, since the wheel building process can be expensive, especially for extension
932	    /// modules.

937	    /// There are three kinds from cache entries: Wheel metadata and policy as `MsgPack` files, the
938	    /// wheels themselves, and the unzipped wheel archives. If a wheel file is over an in-memory
939	    /// size threshold, we first download the zip file into the cache, then unzip it into a
940	    /// directory with the same name (exclusive of the `.whl` extension).
941	    ///
942	    /// Cache structure:
943	    ///  * `wheel-metadata-v0/pypi/foo/{foo-1.0.0-py3-none-any.msgpack, foo-1.0.0-py3-none-any.whl}`
944	    ///  * `wheel-metadata-v0/<digest(index-url)>/foo/{foo-1.0.0-py3-none-any.msgpack, foo-1.0.0-py3-none-any.whl}`
945	    ///  * `wheel-metadata-v0/url/<digest(url)>/foo/{foo-1.0.0-py3-none-any.msgpack, foo-1.0.0-py3-none-any.whl}`
946	    ///
947	    /// See `uv_client::RegistryClient::wheel_metadata` for information on how wheel metadata
948	    /// is fetched.
949	    ///
950	    /// # Example
951	    ///
952	    /// Consider the following `requirements.in`:
953	    /// ```text
954	    /// # pypi wheel
955	    /// pandas
956	    /// # url wheel
957	    /// flask @ https://files.pythonhosted.org/packages/36/42/015c23096649b908c809c69388a805a571a3bea44362fe87e33fc3afa01f/flask-3.0.0-py3-none-any.whl
958	    /// ```
959	    ///
960	    /// When we run `pip compile`, it will only fetch and cache the metadata (and cache policy), it
961	    /// doesn't need the actual wheels yet:
962	    /// ```text
963	    /// wheel-v0
964	    /// ├── pypi
965	    /// │   ...
966	    /// │   ├── pandas
967	    /// │   │   └── pandas-2.1.3-cp310-cp310-manylinux_2_17_x86_64.manylinux2014_x86_64.msgpack
968	    /// │   ...
969	    /// └── url
970	    ///     └── 4b8be67c801a7ecb
971	    ///         └── flask
972	    ///             └── flask-3.0.0-py3-none-any.msgpack
973	    /// ```
974	    ///
975	    /// We get the following `requirement.txt` from `pip compile`:
976	    ///
977	    /// ```text
978	    /// [...]
979	    /// flask @ https://files.pythonhosted.org/packages/36/42/015c23096649b908c809c69388a805a571a3bea44362fe87e33fc3afa01f/flask-3.0.0-py3-none-any.whl
980	    /// [...]
981	    /// pandas==2.1.3
982	    /// [...]
983	    /// ```
984	    ///
985	    /// If we run `pip sync` on `requirements.txt` on a different machine, it also fetches the
986	    /// wheels:
987	    ///
988	    /// TODO(konstin): This is still wrong, we need to store the cache policy too!
989	    /// ```text
990	    /// wheel-v0
991	    /// ├── pypi
992	    /// │   ...
993	    /// │   ├── pandas
994	    /// │   │   ├── pandas-2.1.3-cp310-cp310-manylinux_2_17_x86_64.manylinux2014_x86_64.whl
995	    /// │   │   ├── pandas-2.1.3-cp310-cp310-manylinux_2_17_x86_64.manylinux2014_x86_64
996	    /// │   ...
997	    /// └── url
998	    ///     └── 4b8be67c801a7ecb
999	    ///         └── flask
1000	    ///             └── flask-3.0.0-py3-none-any.whl
1001	    ///                 ├── flask
1002	    ///                 │   └── ...
1003	    ///                 └── flask-3.0.0.dist-info
1004	    ///                     └── ...
1005	    /// ```
1006	    ///
1007	    /// If we run first `pip compile` and then `pip sync` on the same machine, we get both:
1008	    ///
1009	    /// ```text
1010	    /// wheels-v0
1011	    /// ├── pypi
1012	    /// │   ├── ...
1013	    /// │   ├── pandas
1014	    /// │   │   ├── pandas-2.1.3-cp312-cp312-manylinux_2_17_x86_64.manylinux2014_x86_64.msgpack

1020	    /// │   │       │   ├── ...
1021	    /// │   │       └── pandas.libs
1022	    /// │   ├── ...
1023	    /// └── url
1024	    ///     └── 4b8be67c801a7ecb
1025	    ///         └── flask
1026	    ///             ├── flask-3.0.0-py3-none-any.msgpack

12	/// Indicates how the preview was initialised, to distinguish between normal
13	/// code and unit tests.
14	enum PreviewMode {
15	    /// Initialised by a call to [`init`].
16	    Normal(Preview),
17	    /// Initialised by a call to [`test::with_features`].
18	    #[cfg(feature = "testing")]
19	    Test(std::sync::RwLock<Option<Preview>>),
20	}
21	
22	static PREVIEW: OnceLock<PreviewMode> = OnceLock::new();
23	
24	/// Error returned when [`init`] is called more than once.
25	#[derive(Debug, Error)]
26	#[error("The preview configuration has already been initialized")]
27	pub struct InitError;
28	
29	/// Initialize the global preview configuration.
30	///
31	/// This should be called once at startup with the resolved preview settings.
32	pub fn init(preview: Preview) -> Result<(), InitError> {
33	    PREVIEW
34	        .set(PreviewMode::Normal(preview))
35	        .map_err(|_| InitError)
36	}
37	
38	/// Get the current global preview configuration.
39	///
40	/// # Panics
41	///
42	/// When called before [`init`] or (with the `testing` feature) when the
43	/// current thread does not hold a [`test::with_features`] guard.
44	pub fn get() -> Preview {
45	    match PREVIEW.get() {
46	        Some(PreviewMode::Normal(preview)) => *preview,
47	        #[cfg(feature = "testing")]
48	        Some(PreviewMode::Test(rwlock)) => {
49	            assert!(
50	                test::HELD.get(),
51	                "The preview configuration is in test mode but the current thread does not hold a `FeaturesGuard`\nHint: Use `{}::test::with_features` to get a `FeaturesGuard` and hold it when testing functions which rely on the global preview state",
52	                module_path!()
53	            );
54	            // The unwrap may panic only if the current thread had panicked
55	            // while attempting to write the value and then recovered with
56	            // `catch_unwind`. This seems unlikely.
57	            rwlock
58	                .read()
59	                .unwrap()
60	                .expect("FeaturesGuard is held but preview value is not set")
61	        }
62	        #[cfg(feature = "testing")]
63	        None => panic!(
64	            "The preview configuration has not been initialized\nHint: Use `{}::init` or `{}::test::with_features` to initialize it",
65	            module_path!(),
66	            module_path!()
67	        ),
68	        #[cfg(not(feature = "testing"))]
69	        None => panic!("The preview configuration has not been initialized"),
70	    }
71	}
72	
73	/// Check if a specific preview feature is enabled globally.
74	pub fn is_enabled(flag: PreviewFeature) -> bool {
75	    get().is_enabled(flag)
76	}

1033	    Wheels,
1034	    /// Source distributions, wheels built from source distributions, their extracted metadata, and the
1035	    /// cache policy of the source distribution.
1036	    ///
1037	    /// The structure is similar of that of the `Wheel` bucket, except we have an additional layer
1038	    /// for the source distribution filename and the metadata is at the source distribution-level,
1039	    /// not at the wheel level.
1040	    ///
1041	    /// TODO(konstin): The cache policy should be on the source distribution level, the metadata we
1042	    /// can put next to the wheels as in the `Wheels` bucket.
1043	    ///
1044	    /// The unzipped source distribution is stored in a directory matching the source distribution
1045	    /// archive name.
1046	    ///
1047	    /// Source distributions are built into zipped wheel files (as PEP 517 specifies) and unzipped
1048	    /// lazily before installing. So when resolving, we only build the wheel and store the archive
1049	    /// file in the cache, when installing, we unpack it under the same name (exclusive of the
1050	    /// `.whl` extension). You may find a mix of wheel archive zip files and unzipped wheel
1051	    /// directories in the cache.
1052	    ///
1053	    /// Cache structure:
1054	    ///  * `built-wheels-v0/pypi/foo/34a17436ed1e9669/{manifest.msgpack, metadata.msgpack, foo-1.0.0.zip, foo-1.0.0-py3-none-any.whl, ...other wheels}`

1056	    ///  * `built-wheels-v0/url/<digest(url)>/foo/foo-1.0.0.zip/{manifest.msgpack, metadata.msgpack, foo-1.0.0-py3-none-any.whl, ...other wheels}`

Design an implementation plan for adding a `uv cache stats` subcommand to the uv Rust project. The command should show per-bucket breakdown of cache usage with a total at the end, supporting a `--human` flag for human-readable sizes.

## Existing Architecture

### CLI Layer (`crates/uv-cli/src/lib.rs`)
- `CacheCommand` enum (line 878) has variants: `Clean`, `Prune`, `Dir`, `Size`
- `SizeArgs` struct (line 947) has a `human: bool` field with `--human`/`-H`/`--human-readable`
- Need to add a new `Stats(StatsArgs)` variant to `CacheCommand`

### Command Dispatch (`crates/uv/src/lib.rs`)
- Line 1179-1181: `CacheCommand::Size(args)` dispatches to `commands::cache_size(&cache, args.human, printer, globals.preview)`
- Need similar dispatch for the new `Stats` variant

### Command Implementation (`crates/uv/src/commands/cache_size.rs`)
- Uses `diskus::DiskUsage` to compute total size of `cache.root()`
- Uses `human_readable_bytes` from `uv_console` for formatting
- Gated behind `PreviewFeature::CacheSize` preview flag
- Outputs to `printer.stdout_important()`

### Cache Buckets (`crates/uv-cache/src/lib.rs`)
- `CacheBucket` enum (line 934) has 11 variants: Wheels, SourceDistributions, FlatIndex, Git, Interpreter, Simple, Archive, Builds, Environments, Python, Binaries
- `CacheBucket::iter()` (line 1316) returns all buckets EXCEPT Python
- `to_str()` maps to directory names: `wheels-v6`, `sdists-v9`, `flat-index-v2`, `git-v0`, `interpreter-v4`, `simple-v21`, `archive-v0`, `builds-v0`, `environments-v2`, `python-v0`, `binaries-v0`
- `Display` impl (line 1334) uses `to_str()` - so displays the versioned directory name
- `cache.bucket(bucket)` returns the PathBuf for a bucket's directory
- `cache.root()` returns the root cache path

### Preview Feature (`crates/uv-preview/src/lib.rs`)
- `PreviewFeature::CacheSize = 1 << 11` - existing preview feature for cache size
- We should reuse this for cache stats OR add a new one

### Commands module (`crates/uv/src/commands/mod.rs`)
- Line 21: `pub(crate) use cache_size::cache_size;`
- Line 84: `mod cache_size;`
- Need to add similar entries for `cache_stats`

### Test Infrastructure
- Tests at `crates/uv/tests/it/cache_size.rs`
- Test module registered in `crates/uv/tests/it/main.rs` line 27
- `TestContext` has `cache_size()` helper at `crates/uv-test/src/lib.rs:1644`
- `with_filtered_cache_size()` at line 241 adds filters for size values in snapshots

### Formatting
- `human_readable_bytes(bytes: u64) -> (f32, &'static str)` from `uv_console` - uses binary units (KiB, MiB, GiB)
- Format: `"{bytes:.1}{unit}"` (e.g., `384.2KiB`)

## Requirements
1. New `uv cache stats` subcommand
2. Per-bucket breakdown showing each bucket name and its size
3. Total at the end
4. `--human` flag for human-readable sizes (same as cache size)
5. Wire through all layers: CLI args → dispatch → implementation → output
6. Reuse `CacheBucket::iter()` and `cache.bucket()` for bucket paths
7. Use `diskus::DiskUsage` for per-bucket disk usage calculation
8. Add integration tests following the pattern of cache_size tests
9. Behind same preview feature as cache size (or add new one)

## Design Considerations
- Should we use `DiskUsage::new()` per bucket (11 separate calls) or compute once and break down?
  - Per-bucket approach is simpler and follows existing pattern
  - Could also compute all bucket paths in one `DiskUsage::new(vec![...all bucket paths...])` call but that gives a single total, not per-bucket
  - Best approach: iterate over buckets, compute each separately with `DiskUsage`, then sum for total
- Output format: consider table-like alignment for readability
- The `CacheBucket::Display` shows versioned dir names like `wheels-v6` - for user-facing output, consider human-friendly names
- `CacheBucket::iter()` excludes Python - should we include it? Probably yes for stats to be accurate

input.content:
# Plan: Add `uv cache stats` subcommand

## Context

`uv cache size` shows total cache size as a single number. Users want a per-bucket breakdown to understand which parts of the cache consume the most space. This plan adds a new `uv cache stats` subcommand that lists each cache bucket's size individually, with a total at the end, and supports a `--human` flag for human-readable formatting.

## Files to modify/create

| File | Action |
|------|--------|
| `crates/uv-cli/src/lib.rs` | Modify — add `Stats(StatsArgs)` variant + `StatsArgs` struct |
| `crates/uv-cache/src/lib.rs` | Modify — add `display_name()` and `iter_all()` to `CacheBucket` |
| `crates/uv/src/commands/cache_stats.rs` | **Create** — core implementation |
| `crates/uv/src/commands/mod.rs` | Modify — register module + re-export |
| `crates/uv/src/lib.rs` | Modify — add dispatch arm |
| `crates/uv-test/src/lib.rs` | Modify — add `cache_stats()` helper + filter method |
| `crates/uv/tests/it/cache_stats.rs` | **Create** — integration tests |
| `crates/uv/tests/it/main.rs` | Modify — register test module |

## Step-by-step implementation

### Step 1: Add CLI definition (`crates/uv-cli/src/lib.rs`)

**A)** Add variant to `CacheCommand` enum after `Size(SizeArgs)` (line 904):
```rust
/// Show a per-bucket breakdown of cache usage.
///
/// Displays the size of each cache bucket individually, followed by the total. By default,
/// outputs sizes in raw bytes; use `--human` for human-readable output.
Stats(StatsArgs),
```

**B)** Add `StatsArgs` struct after `SizeArgs` (after line 952):
```rust
#[derive(Args, Debug)]
pub struct StatsArgs {
    /// Display sizes in human-readable format (e.g., `1.2 GiB` instead of raw bytes).
    #[arg(long = "human", short = 'H', alias = "human-readable")]
    pub human: bool,
}
```

### Step 2: Add helper methods to `CacheBucket` (`crates/uv-cache/src/lib.rs`)

**A)** Add `display_name()` method inside `impl CacheBucket` (after `to_str()`, ~line 1202):
```rust
/// Returns a human-friendly name for this cache bucket, suitable for user-facing output.
pub fn display_name(self) -> &'static str {
    match self {
        Self::Wheels => "Wheels",
        Self::SourceDistributions => "Source distributions",
        Self::FlatIndex => "Flat index",
        Self::Git => "Git",
        Self::Interpreter => "Interpreter",
        Self::Simple => "Simple",
        Self::Archive => "Archive",
        Self::Builds => "Builds",
        Self::Environments => "Environments",
        Self::Python => "Python",
        Self::Binaries => "Binaries",
    }
}
```

**B)** Add `iter_all()` method after `iter()` (~line 1331). The existing `iter()` excludes `Python`; the new method includes all 11 buckets:
```rust
/// Return an iterator over all cache buckets, including [`CacheBucket::Python`].
pub fn iter_all() -> impl Iterator<Item = Self> {
    [
        Self::Wheels,
        Self::SourceDistributions,
        Self::FlatIndex,
        Self::Git,
        Self::Interpreter,
        Self::Simple,
        Self::Archive,
        Self::Builds,
        Self::Environments,
        Self::Python,
        Self::Binaries,
    ]
    .iter()
    .copied()
}
```

### Step 3: Create command implementation (`crates/uv/src/commands/cache_stats.rs`)

New file. Uses `diskus::DiskUsage` per bucket (same crate as `cache_size`), iterates `CacheBucket::iter_all()`, sums for total. Gated behind `PreviewFeature::CacheSize` (reuse existing feature flag).

Key logic:
- For each bucket: get `cache.bucket(bucket)` path, check `.exists()`, compute size with `DiskUsage`
- Format as `"BucketDisplayName: <size>"` per line
- End with `"Total: <size>"` line
- When `human_readable`: use `human_readable_bytes()` from `uv_console`; special-case 0 as `"0B"`
- When cache root doesn't exist: print all zeros + total zero

### Step 4: Register module (`crates/uv/src/commands/mod.rs`)

- Add `mod cache_stats;` near line 84 (alongside `mod cache_size;`)
- Add `pub(crate) use cache_stats::cache_stats;` near line 21 (alongside `cache_size` re-export)

### Step 5: Wire up dispatch (`crates/uv/src/lib.rs`)

After line 1181 (the `CacheCommand::Size` arm), add:
```rust
Commands::Cache(CacheNamespace {
    command: CacheCommand::Stats(args),
}) => commands::cache_stats(&cache, args.human, printer, globals.preview),
```

### Step 6: Add test helpers (`crates/uv-test/src/lib.rs`)

**A)** Add `cache_stats()` method after `cache_size()` (~line 1649):
```rust
/// Create a `uv cache stats` command.
pub fn cache_stats(&self) -> Command {
    let mut command = self.new_command();
    command.arg("cache").arg("stats");
    self.add_shared_options(&mut command, false);
    command
}
```

**B)** Add `with_filtered_cache_stats()` method after `with_filtered_cache_size()` (~line 251):
```rust
/// Add extra filtering for cache stats output
#[must_use]
pub fn with_filtered_cache_stats(mut self) -> Self {
    // Filter "Label: <raw bytes>" lines
    self.filters
        .push((r"(?m): \d+$".to_string(), ": [SIZE]".to_string()));
    // Filter "Label: <human readable>" lines (e.g., ": 1.2MiB")
    self.filters.push((
        r"(?m): \d+(\.\d+)?[KMGTPE]iB$".to_string(),
        ": [SIZE]".to_string(),
    ));
    // Filter "Label: 0B" lines
    self.filters
        .push((r"(?m): 0B$".to_string(), ": [SIZE]".to_string()));
    self
}
```

### Step 7: Create integration tests (`crates/uv/tests/it/cache_stats.rs`)

New file with three tests (mirroring `cache_size.rs` pattern):
1. `cache_stats_empty_raw` — clean cache, verify all buckets show `0`, total shows `0`
2. `cache_stats_with_packages_raw` — install `iniconfig`, verify output has all bucket lines + total (filtered with `[SIZE]`)
3. `cache_stats_with_packages_human` — same with `--human` flag

### Step 8: Register test module (`crates/uv/tests/it/main.rs`)

After line 27 (`mod cache_size;`), add:
```rust
#[cfg(all(feature = "test-python", feature = "test-pypi"))]
mod cache_stats;
```

## Design decisions

- **Per-bucket `DiskUsage` calls**: 11 separate calls (one per bucket). `diskus` doesn't support per-path breakdowns from a single scan. Performance is acceptable since disk usage scanning is I/O-bound and caches are typically modest in size.
- **Reuse `PreviewFeature::CacheSize`**: No new preview feature needed — `cache stats` is closely related to `cache size`.
- **`iter_all()` vs `iter()`**: Existing `iter()` excludes `Python` (by design for removal ops). Stats needs all buckets for accurate accounting, so we add `iter_all()`.
- **Total = sum of buckets**: Slightly less than `uv cache size` (which scans the root including lock files, .gitignore, etc.), but more semantically meaningful for a per-bucket breakdown.
- **Output format**: `"BucketName: size"` per line, `"Total: size"` at the end. Clean, parseable, no alignment padding needed.

## Verification

1. Run the specific integration tests:
   ```
   cargo test -p uv --test it -- cache_stats --features test-python,test-pypi
   ```
2. Manual smoke test:
   ```
   cargo run -- cache stats --preview --human
   ```

```rust
        r"(?m): \d+(\.\d+)?[KMGTPE]iB$".to_string(),
        ": [SIZE]".to_string(),
    ));
    // Filter "Label: 0B" lines
    self.filters
        .push((r"(?m): 0B$".to_string(), ": [SIZE]".to_string()));
    self
}
```

### Step 7: Create integration tests (`crates/uv/tests/it/cache_stats.rs`)

New file with three tests (mirroring `cache_size.rs` pattern):

1. `cache_stats_empty_raw` — clean cache, verify all buckets show `0`, total shows `0`
2. `cache_stats_with_packages_raw` — install `iniconfig`, verify output has all bucket lines + total (filtered with `[SIZE]`)
3. `cache_stats_with_packages_human` — same with `--human` flag

### Step 8: Register test module (`crates/uv/tests/it/main.rs`)

After line 27 (`mod cache_size;`), add:

```rust
#[cfg(all(feature = "test-python", feature = "test-pypi"))]
mod cache_stats;
```

## Design decisions

- **Per-bucket `DiskUsage` calls**: 11 separate calls (one per bucket). `diskus` doesn't support per-path breakdowns from a single scan. Performance is acceptable since disk usage scanning is I/O-bound and caches are typically modest in size.
- **Reuse `PreviewFeature::CacheSize`**: No new preview feature needed — `cache stats` is closely related to `cache size`.
- **`iter_all()` vs `iter()`**: Existing `iter()` excludes `Python` (by design for removal ops). Stats needs all buckets for accurate accounting, so we add `iter_all()`.
- **Total = sum of buckets**: Slightly less than `uv cache size` (which scans the root including lock files, .gitignore, etc.), but more semantically meaningful for a per-bucket breakdown.
- **Output format**: `"BucketName: size"` per line, `"Total: size"` at the end. Clean, parseable, no alignment padding needed.

## Verification

1. Run the specific integration tests:
   ```
   cargo test -p uv --test it -- cache_stats --features test-python,test-pypi
   ```
2. Manual smoke test:
   ```
   cargo run -- cache stats --preview --human
   ```

tool_input.plan:
# Plan: Add `uv cache stats` subcommand

## Context

`uv cache size` shows total cache size as a single number. Users want a per-bucket breakdown to understand which parts of the cache consume the most space. This plan adds a new `uv cache stats` subcommand that lists each cache bucket's size individually, with a total at the end, and supports a `--human` flag for human-readable formatting.

## Files to modify/create

| File                                    | Action                                                          |
| --------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `crates/uv-cli/src/lib.rs`              | Modify — add `Stats(StatsArgs)` variant + `StatsArgs` struct    |
| `crates/uv-cache/src/lib.rs`            | Modify — add `display_name()` and `iter_all()` to `CacheBucket` |
| `crates/uv/src/commands/cache_stats.rs` | **Create** — core implementation                                |
| `crates/uv/src/commands/mod.rs`         | Modify — register module + re-export                            |
| `crates/uv/src/lib.rs`                  | Modify — add dispatch arm                                       |
| `crates/uv-test/src/lib.rs`             | Modify — add `cache_stats()` helper + filter method             |
| `crates/uv/tests/it/cache_stats.rs`     | **Create** — integration tests                                  |
| `crates/uv/tests/it/main.rs`            | Modify — register test module                                   |

## Step-by-step implementation

### Step 1: Add CLI definition (`crates/uv-cli/src/lib.rs`)

**A)** Add variant to `CacheCommand` enum after `Size(SizeArgs)` (line 904):

```rust
/// Show a per-bucket breakdown of cache usage.
///
/// Displays the size of each cache bucket individually, followed by the total. By default,
/// outputs sizes in raw bytes; use `--human` for human-readable output.
Stats(StatsArgs),
```

**B)** Add `StatsArgs` struct after `SizeArgs` (after line 952):

```rust
#[derive(Args, Debug)]
pub struct StatsArgs {
    /// Display sizes in human-readable format (e.g., `1.2 GiB` instead of raw bytes).
    #[arg(long = "human", short = 'H', alias = "human-readable")]
    pub human: bool,
}
```

### Step 2: Add helper methods to `CacheBucket` (`crates/uv-cache/src/lib.rs`)

**A)** Add `display_name()` method inside `impl CacheBucket` (after `to_str()`, ~line 1202):

```rust
/// Returns a human-friendly name for this cache bucket, suitable for user-facing output.
pub fn display_name(self) -> &'static str {
    match self {
        Self::Wheels => "Wheels",
        Self::SourceDistributions => "Source distributions",
        Self::FlatIndex => "Flat index",
        Self::Git => "Git",
        Self::Interpreter => "Interpreter",
        Self::Simple => "Simple",
        Self::Archive => "Archive",
        Self::Builds => "Builds",
        Self::Environments => "Environments",
        Self::Python => "Python",
        Self::Binaries => "Binaries",
    }
}
```

**B)** Add `iter_all()` method after `iter()` (~line 1331). The existing `iter()` excludes `Python`; the new method includes all 11 buckets:

```rust
/// Return an iterator over all cache buckets, including [`CacheBucket::Python`].
pub fn iter_all() -> impl Iterator<Item = Self> {
    [
        Self::Wheels,
        Self::SourceDistributions,
        Self::FlatIndex,
        Self::Git,
        Self::Interpreter,
        Self::Simple,
        Self::Archive,
        Self::Builds,
        Self::Environments,
        Self::Python,
        Self::Binaries,
    ]
    .iter()
    .copied()
}
```

### Step 3: Create command implementation (`crates/uv/src/commands/cache_stats.rs`)

New file. Uses `diskus::DiskUsage` per bucket (same crate as `cache_size`), iterates `CacheBucket::iter_all()`, sums for total. Gated behind `PreviewFeature::CacheSize` (reuse existing feature flag).

Key logic:

- For each bucket: get `cache.bucket(bucket)` path, check `.exists()`, compute size with `DiskUsage`
- Format as `"BucketDisplayName: <size>"` per line
- End with `"Total: <size>"` line
- When `human_readable`: use `human_readable_bytes()` from `uv_console`; special-case 0 as `"0B"`
- When cache root doesn't exist: print all zeros + total zero

### Step 4: Register module (`crates/uv/src/commands/mod.rs`)

- Add `mod cache_stats;` near line 84 (alongside `mod cache_size;`)
- Add `pub(crate) use cache_stats::cache_stats;` near line 21 (alongside `cache_size` re-export)

### Step 5: Wire up dispatch (`crates/uv/src/lib.rs`)

After line 1181 (the `CacheCommand::Size` arm), add:

```rust
Commands::Cache(CacheNamespace {
    command: CacheCommand::Stats(args),
}) => commands::cache_stats(&cache, args.human, printer, globals.preview),
```

### Step 6: Add test helpers (`crates/uv-test/src/lib.rs`)

**A)** Add `cache_stats()` method after `cache_size()` (~line 1649):

```rust
/// Create a `uv cache stats` command.
pub fn cache_stats(&self) -> Command {
    let mut command = self.new_command();
    command.arg("cache").arg("stats");
    self.add_shared_options(&mut command, false);
    command
}
```

**B)** Add `with_filtered_cache_stats()` method after `with_filtered_cache_size()` (~line 251):

```rust
/// Add extra filtering for cache stats output
#[must_use]
pub fn with_filtered_cache_stats(mut self) -> Self {
    // Filter "Label: <raw bytes>" lines
    self.filters
        .push((r"(?m): \d+$".to_string(), ": [SIZE]".to_string()));
    // Filter "Label: <human readable>" lines (e.g., ": 1.2MiB")
    self.filters.push((
        r"(?m): \d+(\.\d+)?[KMGTPE]iB$".to_string(),
        ": [SIZE]".to_string(),
    ));
    // Filter "Label: 0B" lines
    self.filters
        .push((r"(?m): 0B$".to_string(), ": [SIZE]".to_string()));
    self
}
```

### Step 7: Create integration tests (`crates/uv/tests/it/cache_stats.rs`)

New file with three tests (mirroring `cache_size.rs` pattern):

1. `cache_stats_empty_raw` — clean cache, verify all buckets show `0`, total shows `0`
2. `cache_stats_with_packages_raw` — install `iniconfig`, verify output has all bucket lines + total (filtered with `[SIZE]`)
3. `cache_stats_with_packages_human` — same with `--human` flag

### Step 8: Register test module (`crates/uv/tests/it/main.rs`)

After line 27 (`mod cache_size;`), add:

```rust
#[cfg(all(feature = "test-python", feature = "test-pypi"))]
mod cache_stats;
```

## Design decisions

- **Per-bucket `DiskUsage` calls**: 11 separate calls (one per bucket). `diskus` doesn't support per-path breakdowns from a single scan. Performance is acceptable since disk usage scanning is I/O-bound and caches are typically modest in size.
- **Reuse `PreviewFeature::CacheSize`**: No new preview feature needed — `cache stats` is closely related to `cache size`.
- **`iter_all()` vs `iter()`**: Existing `iter()` excludes `Python` (by design for removal ops). Stats needs all buckets for accurate accounting, so we add `iter_all()`.
- **Total = sum of buckets**: Slightly less than `uv cache size` (which scans the root including lock files, .gitignore, etc.), but more semantically meaningful for a per-bucket breakdown.
- **Output format**: `"BucketName: size"` per line, `"Total: size"` at the end. Clean, parseable, no alignment padding needed.

## Verification

1. Run the specific integration tests:
   ```
   cargo test -p uv --test it -- cache_stats --features test-python,test-pypi
   ```
2. Manual smoke test:
   ```
   cargo run -- cache stats --preview --human
   ```
